content_markdown:
Two resistors, made of the same material, are connected in series to a battery. The length of resistor X is twice that of resistor Y, and X has twice the cross-sectional area of Y.

Which of the following gives $\frac{\text{resistance of X}}{\text{resistance of Y}}$?

diagram_metadata.description: Two cylindrical resistors X and Y connected in series. Resistor X is visibly longer and thicker than resistor Y.

vibe_explanation: Resistance is defined by $R = \rho \frac{L}{A}$. Since both resistors are made of the same material, $\rho$ is constant. For resistor X, $L_X = 2L_Y$ and $A_X = 2A_Y$. Substituting these into the ratio: $\frac{R_X}{R_Y} = \frac{\rho (2L_Y) / (2A_Y)}{\rho L_Y / A_Y} = \frac{2}{2} = 1$. The increase in length (which increases resistance) is perfectly offset by the increase in cross-sectional area (which decreases resistance).
